summary:
A CT scan should be used as it provides detailed images necessary to accurately diagnose both the internal derangements and associated fractures in temporomandibular joint injuries compared to less informative methods such as panorex views which lack sufficient detail needed here due their broader field-of-view limitations when focusing specifically onto small anatomical regions affected during these types of trauma cases where precise localization matters significantly more than overall skeletal assessment alone could provide reliably without additional imaging modalities being utilized appropriately according contextually speaking clinically pertinent information required from diagnostic perspective regarding specific pathologies present herein described situation thus concluding towards necessity utilizing advanced techniques rather relying solely upon conventional ones available currently at hand given current state knowledge about best practices concerning management approaches adopted globally across various medical disciplines dealing directly related fields concerned primarily focused around head neck area anatomy physiology pathology treatment planning etc...
